sections.instruction: Questions 11-14: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Questions 15-20: Label the map below. Write the correct letter, A-I, next to Questions 15-20.
sections.passage_or_context:
Local council report on traffic and highways
Recreation ground after proposed changes

questions.content: A survey found people’s main concern about traffic in the area was
options: A cuts to public transport.
B poor maintenance of roads.
C changes in the type of traffic.

questions.content: Which change will shortly be made to the cycle path next to the river?
options: A It will be widened.
B It will be extended.
C It will be resurfaced.

questions.content: Plans for a pedestrian crossing have been postponed because
options: A the Post Office has moved.
B the proposed location is unsafe.
C funding is not available at present.

questions.content: On Station Road, notices have been erected
options: A telling cyclists not to leave their bikes outside the station ticket office.
B asking motorists to switch off engines when waiting at the level crossing.
C warning pedestrians to leave enough time when crossing the railway line.
A
B
C
D
E
F
G
H
I

sections.instruction: Questions 21-24: Choose TWO letters, A-E. Questions 25-30: Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G, next to Questions 25-30.

questions.content: Which TWO benefits of city bike-sharing schemes do the students agree are the most important?
options: A reducing noise pollution
B reducing traffic congestion
C improving air quality
D encouraging health and fitness
E making cycling affordable

questions.content: Which TWO things do the students think are necessary for successful bike-sharing schemes?
options: A Bikes should have a GPS system.
B The app should be easy to use.
C Public awareness should be raised.
D Only one scheme should be available.
E There should be a large network of cycle lanes.
A They agree it has been disappointing.
B They think it should be cheaper.
C They are surprised it has been so successful.
D They agree that more investment is required.
E They think the system has been well designed.
F They disagree about the reasons for its success.
G They think it has expanded too quickly.

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
sections.passage_or_context:
THE EXTINCTION OF THE DODO BIRD
The dodo was a large flightless bird which used to inhabit the island of Mauritius.
History
• 1507 – Portuguese ships transporting (31) {{BLANK}} stopped at the island to collect food and water.
• 1638 – The Dutch established a (32) {{BLANK}} on the island.
• They killed the dodo birds for their meat.
• The last one was killed in 1681.
Description
• The only record we have is written descriptions and pictures (possibly unreliable).
• A Dutch painting suggests the dodo was very (33) {{BLANK}}
• The only remaining soft tissue is a dried (34) {{BLANK}}
• Recent studies of a dodo skeleton suggest the birds were capable of rapid (35) {{BLANK}}
• It’s thought they were able to use their small wings to maintain (36) {{BLANK}}
• Their (37) {{BLANK}} was of average size.
• Their sense of (38) {{BLANK}} enabled them to find food.
Reasons for extinction
• Hunting was probably not the main cause.
• Sailors brought dogs and monkeys.
• (39) {{BLANK}} also escaped onto the island and ate the birds’ eggs.
• The arrival of farming meant the (40) {{BLANK}} was destroyed.

questions.content: Notes on the extinction of the dodo bird with blanks (31) to (40).
